incidents.vector: Exploited misconfigured GitHub Actions to inject credential-stealing malware into Trivy and KICS vulnerability scanner releases, compromising CI/CD pipelines of downstream users.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments actively compromised with stolen SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and cryptocurrency wallets; Mandiant expects thousands more victims and widespread follow-on extortion attempts.

incidents.victim: Cloud infrastructure users with Iran-locale systems
incidents.vector: Self-propagating CanisterWorm spread through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, deploying a wiper payload on systems matching Iran's timezone or Farsi locale.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys all data on matching Kubernetes cluster nodes or local machines; attackers used blockchain-based ICP canisters resistant to takedown as command infrastructure.

incidents.victim: IoT device owners / DDoS victims including U.S. Department of Defense
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised 3 million+ IoT devices including routers and cameras, launching hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks with extortion demands.
incidents.impact: DOJ dismantled botnet infrastructure; Aisuru alone issued 200,000+ attack commands; victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses per incident. DoD IP addresses were targeted.

incidents.victim: U.S. government officials, military personnel, journalists
incidents.vector: Phishing messages on Signal and other messaging apps mimicking automated support notices to trick victims into linking attacker devices or surrendering account access.
incidents.impact: Thousands of accounts compromised globally; attackers gained access to messages, contact lists, and used compromised accounts for further phishing. FBI and CISA issued joint advisory.

incidents.vector: Social engineering via messaging apps; malware disguised as legitimate software (Pictory, KeePass, Telegram) connects to Telegram bots for bidirectional command-and-control.
incidents.impact: Full device surveillance including screen/audio recording, file exfiltration, and credential theft. FBI linked this infrastructure directly to the Handala persona used in the Stryker attack.

incidents.victim: iPhone users globally (hundreds of millions running iOS 18)
incidents.vector: DarkSword iOS full-chain exploit leveraging six zero-day vulnerabilities (iOS 18.4–18.7), delivered via watering hole sites; a leaked version appeared on GitHub enabling mass exploitation.
incidents.impact: Full device compromise with exfiltration of messages, credentials, location data, and audio. CISA added exploited vulnerabilities to KEV catalog. Multiple threat actors now using the exploit chain, including a commercial surveillance vendor.

incidents.victim: U.S. persons, businesses, and financial institutions
incidents.vector: SocksEscort residential proxy botnet infected 369,000 home and small business routers since 2020, selling proxy access to criminals who committed bank account takeovers and fraud.
incidents.impact: Millions of dollars in fraud losses including $1M cryptocurrency theft, $700K wire fraud, and $100K military card fraud. DOJ-led international operation seized domains and disrupted the network.

incidents.vector: FCC determined that foreign-manufactured consumer routers pose unacceptable national security risk, citing use by state-sponsored hackers for network surveillance, data exfiltration, and botnet attacks.
incidents.impact: FCC banned all future imports of consumer routers manufactured outside the U.S. unless granted security exemption. Ruling cited Salt Typhoon actors using compromised foreign routers for long-term access to U.S. networks.

incidents.vector: EU sanctioned Iranian cyber contractor Emennet Pasargad (IRGC-linked) for attacks on Charlie Hebdo, 2024 Paris Olympics, Swedish SMS service, and 2020 U.S. election interference.
incidents.impact: EU also sanctioned Chinese cyber contractors Integrity Technology Group (Raptor Train botnet) and i-SOON (espionage campaigns). Sanctions freeze assets and ban EU business dealings with designated entities.

incidents.vector: Abused Microsoft Intune endpoint management device-wipe feature after compromising administrative account; deployed malware to hide activity within corporate Microsoft environment.
incidents.impact: 200,000+ devices wiped across operations in 79 countries; 5,000+ workers sent home in Ireland; surgeries cancelled due to unavailable implants; hospitals disconnected Stryker systems. DOJ seized Handala domains; CISA issued endpoint management hardening advisory.

incidents.vector: Compromised administrative account and deployed Pay2Key ransomware days later, encrypting the environment and clearing all event logs to erase traces of activity.
incidents.impact: Healthcare environment encrypted; no evidence of data exfiltration, suggesting strategic disruption rather than financial motivation. Activity coincided with onset of U.S.-Iran military conflict.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, government, hospitality, and education organizations
incidents.vector: Multi-stage fileless phishing campaign using copyright infringement notice lures to deliver PureLog Stealer via in-memory execution with AMSI bypass and anti-VM evasion.
incidents.impact: Harvests Chrome credentials, browser extensions, cryptocurrency wallets, and system information. Campaign primarily targets Germany and Canada but extends to the U.S. and Australia.
